Container.Name: PMDA
Container.Overview:
PMDA (pronounced "pimda") is my take on the self-hosted music server — born from a deep love of music and a lot of frustration with everything else out there.
I wanted a library that actually understands my collection: it scans huge folders, matches every album against MusicBrainz (including a smart soft / release-group pass that rescues the covers and metadata other tools give up on), keeps the tags and artwork you trust, quietly quarantines duplicates and incomplete rips, and publishes a fast, gorgeous, PostgreSQL-backed library you can truly enjoy — real playback with history, cross-user recommendations, rich clickable artist pages, synced lyrics and waveforms, and a clean "matched" library on disk. 100% self-hosted: no cloud lock-in, no phoning home, no AI tokens baked in. Your music, your server, your rules.
Native iOS and Android companion apps let you stream and download your library — lossless FLAC included — anywhere.
This is for people who are in love with music. If that's you, welcome. 🎵

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the PMDA web UI.

Config.Name: Config Directory
Config.Description: Persistent PMDA data: PostgreSQL, Redis state, settings, auth state, logs, and generated cache.

Config.Name: Music Root
Config.Description: Parent music share. Configure the exact Standard and Incoming source folders later in PMDA Settings.

Config.Name: Review Root
Config.Description: Root folder used for duplicate and incomplete review targets.

Config.Name: Enable PMDA Auth
Config.Description: Enable PMDA accounts and session-based access control.

Config.Name: Trust Proxy Headers
Config.Description: Enable when PMDA is placed behind Cloudflare Tunnel or another reverse proxy.

Config.Name: Secure Session Cookies
Config.Description: Set to true when PMDA is served over HTTPS.

Config.Name: Allow Public Bootstrap
Config.Description: Leave false for normal installs. Only enable temporarily if first-user setup must happen from outside your LAN.
